mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.33% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Persistent FEDFUNDS above 4.25% signals sustained policy tightness that keeps real rates elevated, supporting higher 10-year Treasury yields through the term premium and expectations of continued restrictive policy over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 4.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.1% combined with payroll employment at 159,724 thousand indicates a tight labor market. If UNRATE remains below 4.3% and PAYEMS growth continues, wage pressure persists, driving inflation expectations higher and pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as markets price in sustained price pressures.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.3% and PAYEMS month-over-month growth positive
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points despite tight labor market conditions, or correlation between UNRATE-PAYEMS joint signal and 20d yield changes drops below 0.10 over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 715,417 million and housing starts (HOUST) at 1,256 thousand indicate solid aggregate demand. Strong demand signals compress the probability of near-term recession, reducing safe-haven demand for long-duration Treasuries and pushing yields higher as risk appetite increases over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth positive AND HOUST above 1,200 thousand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines despite both RSAFS and HOUST meeting trigger conditions, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 103.5948 (2017=100) reflects moderate supply-side capacity utilization. If INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month, supply constraints ease, reducing inflation expectations and pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward as terminal rate expectations decline.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 10 basis points following INDPRO decline trigger, or signal produces negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI (CPIAUCSL) at 321.500 (1982-1984=100) reflects cumulative inflation. If year-over-year CPI growth remains above 3.5%, inflation expectations remain unanchored, forcing the Fed to maintain or raise FEDFUNDS further, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as long-term rate expectations adjust upward over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days despite elevated year-over-year CPI growth, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 23,512.717 billion (2017 dollars) represents the demand foundation. If quarterly GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate, recession risk rises, triggering flight-to-quality flows into long-duration Treasuries and pushing 10-year yields downward over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Annualized quarterly GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days following GDP deceleration trigger, or signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.1% combined with CPI at 321.500 creates a Phillips curve tension. If UNRATE falls below 3.9% while CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth remains above 3.0%, wage-price spiral risk intensifies, forcing expectations of higher terminal rates and pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice policy duration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 3.9% AND year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0%
regime_hint: wage-price spiral risk in tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines despite both trigger conditions met, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 103.5948 combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 159,724 thousand indicates supply-demand balance. If INDPRO growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while PAYEMS growth slows below 100 thousand monthly, supply outpaces demand, reducing inflation pressure and pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3% AND PAYEMS monthly growth falls below 100 thousand
regime_hint: supply-driven disinflation with labor market softening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 12 basis points despite both trigger conditions met, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.
